(French pronunciation: [[sɔsjete dez‿aʁtist(ə) fʁɑ̃sɛ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), meaning "Society of French Artists") is the [association](/wiki/Professional_association "Professional association") of French painters and sculptors established in 1881. Its annual exhibition is called the "Salon des artistes français" (not to be confused with the better-known [Salon](/wiki/Salon_(Paris) "Salon (Paris)"), established in 1667).[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*]

Association under the French law of 1901

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/65/Paris-Salon_%28IA_parissalon1890pt1enau%29.pdf/page1-250px-Paris-Salon_%28IA_parissalon1890pt1enau%29.pdf.jpg)](/wiki/File:Paris-Salon_(IA_parissalon1890pt1enau).pdf)

Société des Artistes Français (c.1890)

The **Société des Artistes Français** (French pronunciation: [[sɔsjete dez‿aʁtist(ə) fʁɑ̃sɛ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), meaning "Society of French Artists") is the [association](/wiki/Professional_association "Professional association") of French painters and sculptors established in 1881. Its annual exhibition is called the "Salon des artistes français" (not to be confused with the better-known [Salon](/wiki/Salon_(Paris) "Salon (Paris)"), established in 1667).[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*]

When the Société was established, it associated all the French artists. Its president was a painter and its vice-president a sculptor. The main task of the Société is to organize the *Salon*, since the French government ceased to do it.

## Secession

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_des_Artistes_Fran%C3%A7ais&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Secession")]

In December 1890 president Bouguereau suggested that the *Salon* should be an exhibition of young, yet unrecognized, artists. [Ernest Meissonier](/wiki/Ernest_Meissonier "Ernest Meissonier"), [Puvis de Chavannes](/wiki/Puvis_de_Chavannes "Puvis de Chavannes"), [Auguste Rodin](/wiki/Auguste_Rodin "Auguste Rodin") and others rejected this proposal and left the organization. They quickly created their own exhibition ([Société Nationale des Beaux-Arts](/wiki/Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_Nationale_des_Beaux-Arts "Société Nationale des Beaux-Arts") in 1899) that was also named the *Salon*, officially *Salon de la Société Nationale des Beaux–Arts*, in short *Salon du Champs de Mars*. The original *Salon* was sometimes called *Salon de Champs-Élysées*, or simply *Salon des artistes français*).

## Presidents of the association since 1881

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_des_Artistes_Fran%C3%A7ais&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Presidents of the association since 1881")]

* 1881: [Antoine-Nicolas Bailly](/wiki/Antoine-Nicolas_Bailly "Antoine-Nicolas Bailly"), architect
* 1891: [Leon Bonnat](/wiki/Leon_Bonnat "Leon Bonnat"), painter
* 1896: [Édouard Detaille](/wiki/%C3%89douard_Detaille "Édouard Detaille"), painter
* 1900: [Jean-Paul Laurens](/wiki/Jean-Paul_Laurens "Jean-Paul Laurens"), painter
* 1901: [William Bouguereau](/wiki/William_Bouguereau "William Bouguereau"), painter
* 1904: [Tony Robert-Fleury](/wiki/Tony_Robert-Fleury "Tony Robert-Fleury"), painter
* 1907: [Henri-Paul Nénot](/wiki/Henri-Paul_N%C3%A9not "Henri-Paul Nénot"), architect
* 1910: [Victor Laloux](/wiki/Victor_Laloux "Victor Laloux"), architect
* 1913: [Antonin Mercie](/wiki/Antonin_Mercie "Antonin Mercie"), sculptor
* 1917: [Francois Flameng](/wiki/Francois_Flameng "Francois Flameng"), painter
* 1920: [Victor Laloux](/wiki/Victor_Laloux "Victor Laloux"), architect
* 1922: [Jules Coutan](/wiki/Jules_Coutan "Jules Coutan"), sculptor
* 1924: [Henri-Paul Nénot](/wiki/Henri-Paul_N%C3%A9not "Henri-Paul Nénot"), architect
* 1926: [Paul Chabas](/wiki/Paul_Chabas "Paul Chabas"), painter
* 1936: [Alphonse Defrasse](/wiki/Alphonse_Defrasse "Alphonse Defrasse"), architect
* 1939: [Albert Tournaire](/w/index.php?title=Albert_Tournaire&action=edit&redlink=1 "Albert Tournaire (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_Tournaire "fr:Albert Tournaire")], architect
* 1941: [Henri Bouchard](/wiki/Henri_Bouchard "Henri Bouchard"), sculptor
* 1945: [Albert Tournaire](/w/index.php?title=Albert_Tournaire&action=edit&redlink=1 "Albert Tournaire (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_Tournaire "fr:Albert Tournaire")], architect
* 1947: [Jules Formigé](/wiki/Jules_Formig%C3%A9 "Jules Formigé"), architect
* 1955: [Georges Labro](/w/index.php?title=Georges_Labro&action=edit&redlink=1 "Georges Labro (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Georges_Labro "fr:Georges Labro")], architect
* 1959: [Jean-Gabriel Goulinat](/w/index.php?title=Jean-Gabriel_Goulinat&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jean-Gabriel Goulinat (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jean-Gabriel_Goulinat "fr:Jean-Gabriel Goulinat")], painter
* 1965: Georges Cheyssial, painter
* 1977: Georges Muguet, sculptor
* 1979: [Paul Ambille](/w/index.php?title=Paul_Ambille&action=edit&redlink=1 "Paul Ambille (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paul_Ambille "fr:Paul Ambille")], painter
* 1981: [Arnaud d'Hauterives](/w/index.php?title=Arnaud_d%27Hauterives&action=edit&redlink=1 "Arnaud d'Hauterives (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arnaud_d%27Hauterives "fr:Arnaud d'Hauterives")], painter
* 1991: [Jean-Marie Zacchi](/w/index.php?title=Jean-Marie_Zacchi&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jean-Marie Zacchi (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jean-Marie_Zacchi "fr:Jean-Marie Zacchi")], painter
* 1994: Jean Campistron, painter
* 1997: Françoise Zig-Tribouilloy, engraver
* 2000: Christian Billet, painter
* 2010: Viviane Guybet, sculptor
* 2013: [Martine Delaleuf](/wiki/Martine_Delaleuf "Martine Delaleuf"), architect

## Present day

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_des_Artistes_Fran%C3%A7ais&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Present day")]

The **Société des Artistes Français** still exists and organizes each year the "Salon des Artistes Français". Its current President is Ms. Martine Delaleuf.
